cards.def: To control, restrain, or curb
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Bridle your tongue / bridle your enthusiasm"
cards.context: Used when a person restrains an emotional impulse, anger, or sudden panic.
cards.vis: Picture a stressed groom trying to put a horse's leather headpiece onto his screaming, out-of-control bride to restrain her.

cards.def: To send away or expel from a place
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Banish negative thoughts from your mind"
cards.context: Appears when an author mentions casting out bad thoughts, or an academic being exiled from elite intellectual circles.
cards.vis: A king stamps a massive red 'BAN' sign onto a peasant's forehead, violently catapulting them out of the kingdom.

cards.def: To reduce in extent or quantity; impose a restriction on
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Curtail spending / curtail a long speech"
cards.context: Tested almost exclusively in sentences detailing budgetary limits, reducing funding, or curtailing personal liberties.
cards.vis: A stylist uses massive shears to cut the tail off a designer tuxedo jacket right on the runway, drastically shortening it.

cards.def: To hinder, restrain, or prevent an action
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Inhibit the growth of bacteria / feel inhibited at a party"
cards.context: Seen in scientific or sociological contexts where an obstacle blocks growth or a chemical stops a reaction.
cards.vis: A giant, invisible hand physically pushes down on a sprinting runner, trapping them inside a tiny glass box.

cards.def: To enclose or confine someone against their will
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Immured in a tiny office cubicle all day"
cards.context: ETS uses this metaphorically for individuals trapped by tradition, old-fashioned social expectations, or physical isolation.
cards.vis: A screaming mime is physically built into a brick wall by a construction crew, leaving only his eyes exposed.

cards.def: To voluntarily cease to keep or claim; give up
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Relinquish the throne / relinquish control of a project"
cards.context: Usually paired with political power, control of an empire, or an executive stepping down.
cards.vis: A weeping man slowly pulls his fingers off a warm, delicious quiche, letting a giant seagull fly away with it.

cards.def: To make something clear; explain
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Elucidate a complicated theory / elucidate the steps"
cards.context: Appears in Text Completions when a new piece of evidence makes a previously mysterious text easy to understand.
cards.vis: A scientist pours a glowing, neon lightbulb liquid down a pitch-black, murky pipe, rendering it crystal clear.

cards.def: Impossible to understand or interpret
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An inscrutable poker face / inscrutable handwriting"
cards.context: Used to describe an historical figure's motivations, an author's obscure writing style, or a stoic leader.
cards.vis: A stone Sphinx wearing dark sunglasses stares blankly at you while holding a completely colorless Rubik's cube.

cards.def: To think deeply about something
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Ruminate over a difficult career choice"
cards.context: Tested when a character reflects on past mistakes or when scholars ponder a difficult philosophical paradox.
cards.vis: A philosophical cow sitting in a library armchair, wearing spectacles, slowly chewing its cud while solving calculus.

cards.def: Affording a general view of a whole; summary
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A synoptic chart / synoptic overview of the event"
cards.context: Commonly paired with 'comprehensive' in Sentence Equivalence. Describing a textbook chapter or report that gives an overview.
cards.vis: A giant, disembodied eyeball looking down from space through a lens that fits Earth's history onto an index card.

cards.def: Vehement or clamorous; crying out noisily
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A vociferous opponent of the new tax laws"
cards.context: Used when critics or opposing political factions loudly, aggressively, and constantly protest against a new policy.
cards.vis: An angry protester shouting so loudly through a megaphone that fire and actual barking dogs shoot out of the speaker.

cards.def: To think out or plan an action beforehand
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Premeditated murder / a premeditated PR stunt"
cards.context: Contrast word to 'spontaneous.' Used when an action looks accidental but was actually meticulously planned behind closed doors.
cards.vis: A supervillain drawing elaborate, color-coded schematics of a bank heist 5 years before they actually steal a cent.

cards.def: Lacking initiative or strength of character; irresponsible
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A feckless employee who keeps sleeping at work"
cards.context: ETS uses this to slam weak-willed leaders, lazy institutions, or efforts that lack determination.
cards.vis: A lazy teenager made entirely out of limp, wet noodles slumping off a couch, failing to even hold up a remote control.

cards.def: Recklessly extravagant or wasteful in the use of resources
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Profligate spending on luxury vacations"
cards.context: Often forms standard GRE contrast questions against thriftiness or parsimony.
cards.vis: A billionaire standing on a golden balcony lighting stacks of bills on fire just to use them as tees to hit diamonds.

cards.def: Excitably frivolous, fickle, or easily scared
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Skittish investors pulling money out of stocks"
cards.context: Typically applied to global stock markets, unpredictable investors, or populations made jumpy by political rumors.
cards.vis: A hyperactive horse wearing roller skates, jumping sky-high every single time a tiny piece of confetti falls.

cards.def: Strange, odd, or distinctive to one person/thing
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A custom peculiar to this remote island"
cards.context: Rarely means 'weird' on the GRE; instead, it means unique to a specific region or specific culture.
cards.vis: A chicken with three heads wearing a tiny top hat, walking a human on a leash down a busy city street.

cards.def: Complete with regard to every detail; meticulous
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A thorough background check / thorough cleanup"
cards.context: Used to describe perfect academic peer-reviews, rigorous laboratory procedures, or exhaustive archival research.
cards.vis: A detective using a microscope and tweezers to individually examine every single dust mote on a three-mile rug.

cards.def: Thinking about or planning the future with imagination
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A visionary leader like Steve Jobs"
cards.context: Appears when praising artists or city planners who anticipate cultural trends decades before their peers do.
cards.vis: An eccentric inventor wearing glowing neon glasses as blueprints of cloud-cities float out of their eyes.

cards.def: Sad and disappointed
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Left crestfallen after losing the match"
cards.context: Tested when researchers discover their life's work or a key hypothesis has just been decisively disproven by a rival.
cards.vis: A proud pirate captain whose feathered hat droops down past his chin as he slumps away after losing his map.

cards.def: To act in accordance with a wish or command
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Comply with court orders / comply with safety regulations"
cards.context: Appears when countries follow international law treaties, or corporations yielding to federal safety rules.
cards.vis: A rigid iron soldier instantly melting into flexible clay the exact second a commander whispers to him.

cards.def: To create and perform spontaneously without preparation
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Improvise a jazz solo / improvise a tool out of a hanger"
cards.context: Appears in prompts celebrating clever solutions built on the fly when standard operational resources completely fail.
cards.vis: A chef whose kitchen burns down suddenly using a hot car engine block to perfectly sear a five-star steak.

cards.def: To restore friendly relations; make consistent
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Reconcile differences / reconcile bank accounts"
cards.context: Extremely common in GRE passages when a scientist figures out how to make two contradictory data points match up beautifully.
cards.vis: A massive earthquake pushing two separated cliffs back together so perfectly that a broken bridge aligns flawlessly.

cards.def: To remove impurities; improve to a fine state
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Refine oil / refine your presentation skills"
cards.context: Used when an author edits their prose over years, polishing a rough early draft into a masterpiece.
cards.vis: A goldsmith filtering river sludge through lasers until a flawless, blindingly bright diamond drop falls out.

cards.def: Attacking or ignoring cherished beliefs or traditions
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An iconoclastic filmmaker who breaks all Hollywood rules"
cards.context: Describes a revolutionary scientist or artist whose work smashes old paradigms and standard academic dogma.
cards.vis: A rebel in a leather jacket riding a motorcycle through a museum, smashing ancient statues with a sledgehammer.

cards.def: To treat someone as a celebrity or give great importance
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Fans lionize pop stars wherever they go"
cards.context: Frequently paired with 'deify' or 'exalt'. Used when society unthinkingly showers an historical figure with endless worship.
cards.vis: A normal librarian steps outside and an adoring crowd instantly drops a golden lion mane onto her shoulders.

cards.def: To precede in time; come before something else
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Dinosaurs antedate the evolution of humans"
cards.context: Appears in history or archaeology sentences where a newly discovered document proves a custom is much older than assumed.
cards.vis: A modern smartphone discovered inside an ancient sealed Egyptian tomb, proving it existed before the pyramids.

cards.def: To escape or avoid, especially by cleverness/trickery
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Evade taxes / evade a difficult question"
cards.context: Used when politicians successfully dodge tough press questions, or species avoiding detection by predators.
cards.vis: A slippery ninja turning into smoke right as police tackle him, leaving them crashing into each other.

cards.def: To become worse or more intense; rot
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Letting a wound fester / bitterness festured inside"
cards.context: Usually tests metaphorically: an ignored political grievance or a hidden social injustice that grows highly toxic over time.
cards.vis: A forgotten green apple in a locker that sprouts purple eyes, oozes slime, and mutters angry words.

cards.def: Dull, tedious, and repetitious; lacking variety
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A monotonous drone / monotonous daily routine"
cards.context: Tested in descriptions of barren desert landscapes, mechanical bureaucratic jobs, or prose styles devoid of inflection.
cards.vis: A factory worker pressing a single gray button every three seconds while a horn emits a flat 'BEEP' for 40 years.

cards.def: Unnecessary, especially through being more than enough
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Superfluous details in an explanation"
cards.context: Paired with 'redundant.' Appears when an editor trims extra paragraphs out of an already complete book essay.
cards.vis: Ten waiters simultaneously pouring giant pitchers of water over a man's head until he is entirely submerged at the table.
